MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kristina Lam appeared by zoom for Austin Thompson.

Mrs. Lam stated that she is comfortable that the parties were advised of their circumstances and rights, that they have a settlement agreement and waivers, and that they are waiting for signatures. The Petition to Terminate Estate is continued to September 9, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-27-2023 Continued To Jan-22-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Rowena Navia appeared via zoom (former counsel) for Keisha Smith(administrator).

Discussion was held regarding Ms. Smith's absence, that she was unable to retain counsel, that talks about closing the conservatorship and transferring everything to the Estate of Roosevelt Thompson, and that there was discussion amongst the family of possibly selling the real property. Petition for letters administration and will annexed is continued to January 22, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-28-2023 Continued To Nov-27-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-18-302457 Estate of Thelma M. Thompson and
PES-20-303966 Estate of Roosevelt Thompson.

Appearances: Kristina Lam appeared by zoom for Keisha Smith (Thelma's Estate) and for Alicia Thompson (Roosevelt's Estate).

Mrs. Lam stated that they have spoken with the family members and that they indicated that they would not be pursuing independent counsel to investigate potential challenges to the joint tenancy issue. Mrs. Lam also stated that they are working with Keisha to file a substitution of attorney, that Keisha will be in pro per, that they believe Keisha will be withdrawing her petition and that they will be filing the substitution within 30-days. In addition, Mrs. Lam stated that the only asset in Roosevelt's estate is the property, that they are working with the probate referee on finalizing the inventory and appraisal, and that she believes they will be asking for more time in his estate because they want to make sure that the conservatorship case is closed. The Petition for Letters of Administration in Thelma's estate is continued to November 27, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate in Roosevelt's estate is continued to November 20, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Sae Phan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-06-2023 Continued To Aug-28-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia via Zoom, for and with Keisha Smith, via Zoom; Roosevelt Thompson, Jr., interested Party.

The court confirms with Ms. Smith that she does not intend to hire independent counsel and advises of her understanding regarding this and the related matter and what she expects. The court confirms that she is aware of the ramifications of not hiring independent counsel and that the court wanted to confirm that the parties have been properly advised of the preceding. Mr. Thompson, Jr., advises that he does not intend to hire independent counsel. Ms. Navia reports of a meeting amongst family members to discuss the related Estate matters, of their respective positions and of attempting to settle the matters without litigation. Mr. Thompson, Jr., confirms Ms. Navia's report. The court confirms with Ms. Navia that she wishes to continue this matter to track the Estate of Roosevelt Thompson.

The matter is taken under submission with the intent to continue it to track the related matter as stated above.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

***After hearing (3/06/2023): after consideration by the court, this matter is continued to Aug-28-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-24-2023 Continued To Mar-06-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance via Zoom: Kristina Lam, Esq. for Keisha Smith (Petitioner).

The Court stated that the judge at the prior hearing had indicated that Ms. Smith was to get some independent advice and that the prior judge's thinking was that Ms. Lam's firm would provide the assistance. Ms. Lam stated that they had provided Ms. Smith with the names of attorneys to contact and their numbers, that they understand that Ms. Smith has a call scheduled with Ms. Robello's firm today, and that a 30-day continuance should be granted to allow time for Ms. Smith to obtain counsel and for the substitution to be filed.

At the request of Ms. Lam, the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is continued to March 6, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Cristobal. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-22-2022 Continued To Jan-24-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Fasesky, via Zoom, specially appearing for Rowena Navia, for Keisha Smith, Petitioner.

The court and counsel discuss the issue of whether the court should allow the firm to continue to represent Keisha Smith as personal representative of this estate and as counsel for the resigning personal representative and Alicia as personal representative of the related Estate of Roosevelt Thompson's estate. Counsel advises of the plan to discuss the preceding with Ms. Navia and the parties in hopes of finding a resolution to the preceding issue. The court advises that it is not inclined to grant this petition without Keisha retaining independent counsel.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Jan-24-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Counsel is instructed to file a status report by Jan-17-2023 with a courtesy copy delivered to the court by Jan-18-2023.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Finding A Private Fiduciary
Off Calendar Nov-30-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia, via ZOOM, for Keisha Smith, Petitioner.

Ms. Navia reports that she has submitted for e-filing a document with six consents to multiple party representation by her firm. The court and Ms. Navia discuss the issue regarding Roosevelt Jr.'s estate and how to determine the ownership percentages of the real property. Ms. Navia states the possibility of filing a petition seeking an order to equally divide ownership of the real property between Roosevelt Jr. and Thelmas's estates but that she would need to time to research if the law would allow the preceding. The idea of filing an 850 petition is also discussed but the court notes that there may be a conflict there which would require hiring a different attorney to handle that matter.

After additional discussion, the Status on Finding a Private Fiduciary is taken off calendar. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to Jan-25-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Finding A Private Fiduciary
Off Calendar Nov-02-2021 Continued To Nov-30-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia, via ZOOM, for Keisha Smith, via ZOOM, Petitioner.
The court queries counsel regarding the status of consents. Ms. Navia reports that she has consents which have yet to be filed. Ms. Navia advises that the main goal now is to clean-up the estate and that the main asset of the estate is real property and she expects to obtain waivers. Ms. Navia also advises that she filed a declaration yesterday to address the Examiner's notes. The court asks if she expects to obtain a waiver from Roosevelt Jr. Ms. Navia advises that Roosevelt Jr. is not a beneficiary of the estate but that she can obtain a waiver. The court advises Ms. Navia that the court prefers having a waiver from Roosevelt Jr. on file.

The Petitions are continued to Nov-30-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Resignation Of Administrator
Off Calendar Aug-10-2021 Continued To Sep-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia, via ZOOM, for Austin Thompson, Executor.

The court begins by asking counsel for an update regarding the resignation of Administrator and search for a successor. Ms. Navia reports that as of the last calling of this matter, she believed that the decedent's daughter would want to take over as Administrator but that the daughter had declined. Ms. Navia reports that she will be discussing this matter with other family members and hopes to find a successor soon. The court points Ms. Navia to its concern with Mr. Thompson continuing in his capacity as Administrator and advises that if a successor Administrator has not been found by the next calling of this matter, the court may need to consider appointing a professional fiduciary to take over as Administrator.

The Status of Resignation of Administrator, and Status of Petition for Successor Administrator are continued to September 28, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Status on Finding a Private Fiduciary is added to the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Petition For Successor Administrator
Off Calendar Aug-10-2021 Continued To Sep-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jun-16-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia, via ZOOM, counsel for Austin Thompson, Administrator.

The court begins by advising counsel that it is concerned with the possible conflict of interest in the related Estate matter wherein Alicia Thompson has been appointed as Personal Representative. Ms. Navia reports that her client has been dealing with health-related issues but wishes to continue to serve as Administrator in this matter and as Conservator in the conservatorship matter, regardless. The court and Ms. Navia then discuss the fact that it appears as though Mr. Thompson at some point soon, may no longer be able to serve in the previously mentioned capacities. Ms. Navia believes that Alicia Thompson would be a valid alternative to replace him. The court then strongly suggests that Ms. Navia advise Mr. Thompson to resign and to find a suitable replacement.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Status Petition is taken under submission. A Status of Resignation of Administrator and Status of a Petition for a Successor Administrator are set for August 10, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-21-2020 Continued To Dec-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for Austin Thompson (Administrator).

Ms. Navia stated that the Administrator has been ill, that there is a companion case (PCN-07-289549 Conservatorship of Roosevelt Thompson), that they have been helping the Administrator complete the accounting in that matter and that subsequently Roosevelt has died. Ms. Navia also stated that an estate is open for Roosevelt (PES-20-303966), that there might be a petition for appointment of a Successor Administrator in Thelma's estate and that it may be the Daughter (Austin's Daughter). In addition, Ms. Navia requested a 60-day continuance stating that she is pressing forward with Austin to file the inventory and appraisal, and to get a report on status of administration. Status on close of estate is continued to December 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Off Calendar Apr-02-2019 Continued To Apr-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

If the Examiner's notes are responded to well in advance of the hearing, the matter may be pre-granted and an appearance may not be required.
